mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0%, labor market deterioration signals imminent corporate earnings contraction, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.05 over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during post-shock recovery phases indicates restored manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which supports earnings revisions and positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery phases do not precede positive 20-day SPX returns, or the mechanism reverses direction in recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and wage growth, which mechanically supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns across multiple market cycles, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth falls below 1.5%, economic stagnation reduces corporate revenue trajectories, prompting institutional deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth drops below 1.5%, invalidating the stagnation penalty signal.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a low federal funds rate compresses real yields and forces markets to price aggressive future tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS interaction fails to predict negative 20-day returns, or the signal loses statistical significance when real yields are positive.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during expansionary phases confirms synchronized supply-side strength, reducing input cost pressures and driving positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during expansions fail to predict positive 5-day SPX returns, or the signal reverses direction in expansionary regimes.
